subheading: Run Ideogram in ComfyUI: an image model built for accurate text rendering and typography, on Comfy Cloud or your own GPU.
extendedDescription:
The reason to reach for Ideogram comfyui workflows is text. Ideogram is an image-generation model from a team of former Google Brain researchers, and its defining strength is rendering legible, correctly spelled words inside an image, the part most generators still get wrong. These workflows wire the model into an editable graph where the prompt and the output pass through nodes you can chain, so the typography and everything around it stay under your control.

What that buys you is design work that depends on type. Put quoted text in your prompt and Ideogram will render it on a poster headline, a product label, a social graphic, or an event flyer, handling multi-line layouts and curved surfaces more reliably than a typical image model. Independent benchmarks put its in-image text accuracy near 90 percent, well above the 30 to 40 percent that some competing generators manage. The model also offers style direction, so the same headline can come out photographic, illustrated, or typographic.

There are two ways to run it, and the variant decides which. Ideogram 3.0 (March 2025) is cloud only and runs through ComfyUI's API nodes, with Style References for up to three guide images, four style types (Auto, General, Realistic, Design), and three speed tiers (Turbo, Default, Quality). Ideogram 4.0, released June 2026, is the first open-weight build: a roughly 9.3B text-to-image model with native 2K output, an NF4 checkpoint that fits a single 24GB GPU like an RTX 4090, and an FP8 build for broader hardware. The catch is the license, the Ideogram Non-Commercial Model Agreement, so a local 4.0 download is for personal and research use, while paid commercial rights come with the API.

To run one, open an Ideogram workflow on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install, or download the 4.0 weights and run the graph on your own GPU. Write a prompt with your text in quotes, choose the aspect ratio, and generate. If the lettering or layout is not quite right, revise the prompt and re-run until the words sit cleanly in the composition, then carry the result into the rest of your ComfyUI workflow.

modelSpec.summary: Ideogram is an image-generation model from former Google Brain researchers, known for industry-leading text rendering and typography, run in ComfyUI through API nodes (3.0) or open weights (4.0).
highlights: Industry-leading text rendering for legible, correctly spelled words in-image
Handles multi-line text layouts and curved surfaces for design work
Independent benchmarks put in-image text accuracy near 90 percent
Ideogram 3.0 is cloud only through ComfyUI API nodes; Style References and four style types
Ideogram 4.0 is open weight: about 9.3B, native 2K, NF4 build fits a 24GB GPU
Style direction so a headline can be photographic, illustrated, or typographic

howToUse: Pick an Ideogram workflow for text-to-image with rendered typography.
Run 3.0 on Comfy Cloud through the API nodes, or download the open-weight 4.0 build for a local GPU.
Write a prompt and put any text you want rendered inside quotation marks.
Choose the aspect ratio and a style direction if the workflow exposes one.
Generate the image and check that the lettering and layout read cleanly.
Revise the prompt and re-run if needed, then download the result.

faqItems.question: What are Ideogram ComfyUI workflows?
faqItems.answer: They are ready-made ComfyUI graphs that run Ideogram, the text-rendering image model, either through ComfyUI's API nodes for 3.0 or from open weights for 4.0. Each wires up a text-to-image task with rendered typography, so you give a prompt and get a result without assembling the graph yourself.

faqItems.question: Why is Ideogram good at rendering text?
faqItems.answer: Ideogram was built by former Google Brain researchers with text rendering as a core focus, so it produces legible, correctly spelled words far more reliably than typical image models. Independent benchmarks put its in-image text accuracy near 90 percent, which makes it a strong fit for posters, labels, and graphics where the words have to be right.

faqItems.question: Can I run Ideogram locally on my own GPU?
faqItems.answer: It depends on the version. Ideogram 4.0, released in June 2026, is open weight, so its NF4 checkpoint runs locally on a single 24GB GPU and there is an FP8 build for broader hardware. Ideogram 3.0 remains cloud only and runs through ComfyUI's API nodes, with no local weights to download.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything to run Ideogram?
faqItems.answer: Not for the cloud path. Ideogram 3.0 runs on Comfy Cloud in the browser with nothing to install, since the API nodes handle the compute. For a local Ideogram 4.0 run you install ComfyUI and download the open-weight model files from Hugging Face into your models folder.

faqItems.question: Does Ideogram cost money to use in ComfyUI?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Comfy Cloud is a paid plan with tiered seats, and the Ideogram API nodes bill per generation, so cloud 3.0 runs draw on credits. Downloading the open-weight 4.0 build avoids per-image charges, but its non-commercial license limits self-hosted use to personal and research work.

faqItems.question: How do I get specific text into an Ideogram image?
faqItems.answer: Put the exact words inside quotation marks in your prompt, and Ideogram will render them in the image. If the layout or spelling is not quite right, revise the prompt and re-run until the lettering sits cleanly in the composition.

faqItems.question: What styles can Ideogram generate?
faqItems.answer: Ideogram offers style direction, so the same prompt can come out photographic, illustrated, or typographic, and 3.0 exposes style types like Auto, General, Realistic, and Design. That lets you match the look to the design, whether you need a realistic product shot or a stylized poster.

faqItems.question: What can I create with Ideogram workflows?
faqItems.answer: These workflows suit design work that depends on typography: poster headlines, product labels, social graphics, and event flyers with clean rendered text. Because the graph is open, the output can flow into other ComfyUI nodes for further editing.
